top_signals.title: How the Iran war is reshaping the EU’s strategy on Russian oil sanctions
top_signals.summary: The European Union is considering freezing its $44.10-per-barrel price cap on Russian oil as the Middle East conflict and disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz drive crude prices sharply higher. As tensions in the Middle East continue to escalate and concerns over global energy supplies mount, the European Union is considering a temporary freeze on ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Morgan Stanley's Weaver Sees Continued AI-Driven Equity Momentum
top_signals.summary: Michelle Weaver of Morgan Stanley discusses the outlook for equities, emphasizing the strong demand for technology driven by AI developments. The 10-year Treasury yield level around 4.5% is seen as critical for equity valuations, with yields above this level potentially weighing on equities. While some inflationary pressures, such as gasoline prices, are causing concern, Weaver views these as relatively narrow and partly linked to geopolitical factors like the Strait of Hormuz tensions, which the president has indicated could persist until September. (Source: Bloomberg)
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.

top_signals.title: Crude oil stocks plunge, signaling strong demand
top_signals.summary: The American Petroleum Institute (API) recently released its latest report on U.S. crude oil inventories, revealing a significant decline in stock levels. According to the API, crude oil inventories fell by 6.750 million barrels, a figure that has caught the attention of market analysts and investors alike. This actual drop in crude oil stocks is ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: SpaceX Said to Target $75 Billion in IPO at $135 Per Share
top_signals.summary: SpaceX is planning to offer shares ​at $135 apiece to raise $75 ​billion in its ⁠initial public ​offering, according to people familiar with the matter, as Elon Musk rejects another Wall Street convention by setting a fixed price ahead of the marketing phase of the deal.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Meta is trying to sell AI agents to businesses in latest effort to diversify away from ads
top_signals.summary: The Meta Business Agent will be included in the company's Meta One paid subscription offerings.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Personal Income and Outlays, April 2026
top_signals.summary: Personal income decreased less than $0.1 billion (less than 0.1 percent at a monthly rate) in April, according to estimates released today by the U.S. Bureau of Economic Analysis (BEA). Disposable personal income (DPI)-personal income less personal current taxes-decreased $19.9 billion (0.1 percent), and personal consumption expenditures (PCE) increased $111.1 billion (0.5 percent). Personal outlays-the sum of PCE, personal interest payments, and personal current transfer payments-increased $114.0 billion in April. Personal saving was $611.7 billion in April, and the personal saving rate-personal saving as a percentage of DPI-was 2.6 percent. Full Text
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.

top_signals.title: GDP (Second Estimate) and Corporate Profits, 1st Quarter 2026
top_signals.summary: Real gross domestic product (GDP) increased at an annual rate of 1.6 percent in the first quarter of 2026 (January, February, and March), according to the second estimate released today by the U.S. Bureau of Economic Analysis. In the fourth quarter of 2025, real GDP increased 0.5 percent. The contributors to the increase in real GDP in the first quarter were exports, investment, consumer spending, and government spending. Imports, which are a subtraction in the calculation of GDP, increased. Full Text
